scenarios.description: element with no childNodes returns an empty Elements list (childNodeSize()==0 branch)
scenarios.precondition: Element el=new Element("div"); // no children; shadowChildrenRef==null

scenarios.description: element with childNodes but no Element instances returns empty list after one filter iteration
scenarios.precondition: Element el=new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new TextNode("text")); // one TextNode, no shadow cache

scenarios.description: element with multiple mixed childNodes filters only Element instances over N iterations
scenarios.precondition: Element parent=new Element("div"); parent.appendChild(new TextNode("t1")); parent.appendChild(new Element("span")); parent.appendChild(new DataNode("d")); parent.appendChild(new Element("p"));
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements contains exactly two Elements in insertion order (span,p)
pseudocode: assert result.size() == 2; assert result.get(0).tagName().equals("span"); assert result.get(1).tagName().equals("p");

scenarios.description: consecutive children() call uses cached shadowChildrenRef when available
scenarios.precondition: Element el=new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new Element("a")); el.appendChild(new Element("b")); // first call builds cache
scenarios.expectedResult: both calls return equal Elements instances; second call avoids loop filter

scenarios.description: after node list change, cache is invalidated and children() rebuilds list (nodelistChanged branch)
scenarios.precondition: Element el=new Element("ul"); el.appendChild(new Element("li")); el.children(); // build cache; then el.appendChild(new Element("li")) triggers nodelistChanged
scenarios.expectedResult: first children size==1; after append, children size==2
